key_features: Replaced impairment-only cost model; requires annual and interim disclosures; cumulative-effect adjustment to retained earnings
Earlier standard applied by default; revaluation model requires active market which is rare for crypto
Applied when crypto held for sale in ordinary course of business (broker-traders)
Confirmed cryptocurrencies are IAS 38 intangible assets or IAS 2 inventory; IASB has not added dedicated crypto standard to workplan
IASB added crypto-asset accounting to research agenda in 2024; convergence with FASB possible
Requires entities that safeguard crypto-assets to recognise a liability; controversial; Congress attempted to overturn via CRA
First comprehensive cross-border crypto regulation; covers issuance, trading, and service providers
Impairment-only model created asymmetric accounting; gains only recognised on sale
